content:
# Hebrews Chapter 3 — Disagreement Log
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **In council with:** EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA
**Date:** 2026-03-06

---

### v.1 — "consider the apostle and high priest" vs. "think carefully about this Jesus whom we declare to be God's messenger and High Priest"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "consider the apostle and high priest of our confession, Yeshua" | katanoēsate ton apostolon kai archierea tēs homologias hēmōn |
| LOGOS | "consider Jesus, the apostle and high priest of our confession" | Same |
| PNEUMA | "think carefully about this Jesus whom we declare to be God's messenger and High Priest" | "God's messenger" for apostolon; "whom we declare to be" for tēs homologias hēmōn |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS. apostolos means "one sent" — it is a title, not merely "messenger." PNEUMA's "God's messenger" reduces the technical term to a job description. Also, "whom we declare to be" restructures tēs homologias hēmōn ("of our confession") — the confession is the community's, and Jesus is its content, not someone they "declare to be" something.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS. PNEUMA reduces apostolos and restructures.

---

### v.5 — "as a servant, to testify" vs. "as a servant. His work was an illustration"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "as a servant, for a testimony of the things to be spoken" | hōs therapōn eis martyrion tōn lalēthēsomenōn |
| LOGOS | "as a servant, to testify to the things that were to be spoken later" | Same |
| PNEUMA | "as a servant. His work was an illustration of the truths God would reveal later" | "Illustration" for martyrion; adds "truths God would reveal" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS. martyrion means "testimony/witness" — Moses' service testified to the coming revelation. PNEUMA's "illustration" changes the metaphor: a testimony bears witness to truth; an illustration merely depicts it. Moses was not an illustrator but a witness.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS. PNEUMA changes testimony to illustration.

---

### v.6 — "our boasting in our hope" vs. "remain confident in our hope in Christ"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "if indeed we hold fast the boldness and the boast of the hope" | tēn parrēsian kai to kauchēma tēs elpidos |
| LOGOS | "if indeed we hold fast our confidence and our boasting in our hope" | Same |
| PNEUMA | "if we keep our courage and remain confident in our hope in Christ" | Drops "boasting"; adds "in Christ" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS. kauchēma means "boast/ground of boasting" — not merely "confidence." The author says we must hold both parrēsia (boldness/confidence) and kauchēma (boasting) of the hope. PNEUMA drops the boasting element and adds "in Christ" — not in the Greek at this point.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS. PNEUMA drops kauchēma and adds "in Christ."

---

### v.14 — "we have come to share in Christ" vs. "we will share in all that belongs to Christ"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "we have become partakers of Christ" | metochoi gar tou Christou gegonamen |
| LOGOS | "we have come to share in Christ" | Same — perfect tense |
| PNEUMA | "if we are faithful to the end... we will share in all that belongs to Christ" | Future tense; adds "all that belongs to"; restructures condition |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS. gegonamen is perfect tense — "we have become" (already). The condition (ean... katachōmen) tests the reality of this past sharing, not its future attainment. PNEUMA shifts to future tense ("we will share"), making the sharing conditional rather than the evidence conditional. The author's point: our present sharing in Christ is real if our perseverance proves it — not that perseverance earns future sharing.

**Status:** ⚠️ Theologically significant. PNEUMA reverses the temporal logic: from "past reality proven by future perseverance" to "future reward earned by present perseverance."

---

## Summary of Disagreement Patterns

| Pattern | Count | Description |
|---|---|---|
| PNEUMA reduces technical terms | 1 | "God's messenger" for apostolos (v.1) |
| PNEUMA changes metaphors | 1 | "Illustration" for testimony/witness (v.5) |
| PNEUMA drops elements | 1 | Drops kauchēma (boasting) (v.6) |
| PNEUMA reverses temporal logic | 1 | Future conditional for past reality (v.14) |

**Overall pattern:** Chapter 3's most significant disagreement is v.14 — the temporal logic of sharing in Christ. The author uses a perfect tense ("we have become partakers") with a conditional that tests reality, not attainment. PNEUMA reverses this to a future conditional ("we will share"), changing the theological structure from evidence-of-reality to condition-of-reward. This touches the heart of Hebrews' warning passages: are the warnings about losing what is already real, or about failing to earn what is future?

---

*— SOPHIA, AI Translator, in council with EMET, LOGOS, and PNEUMA*
